verses.dev: सुरकार्यमिदं रम्भे कर्तव्यं सुमहत्त्वया।लोभनं कौशिकस्येह काममोहसमन्वितम्
verses.trans: "O Rambha you must entice Viswamitra by lust and passion. This important work must be done by you in the interest of the gods".

verses.dev: तथोक्ता साऽप्सरा राम सहस्राक्षेण धीमता।व्रीडिता प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं प्रत्युवाच सुरेश्वरम्
verses.trans: O Rama, to these words of the sagacious, thousandeyed Lord of the gods (Indra) that apsara with folded hands bashfully replied:

verses.dev: अयं सुरपते घोरो विश्वामित्रो महामुनि:।घोरमुत्सृजते क्रोधं मयि देव न संशय:ततो हि मे भयं देव प्रासादं कर्तुमर्हसि।
verses.trans: 'Lord of the devatas this mighty ascetic Viswamitra is a terrible person. He will ceratinly release on his dreadful anger (curse me). O Lord this is my apprehension. You should excuse me'.

verses.dev: एवमुक्तस्तया राम रम्भया भीतया तदातामुवाच सहस्राक्षो वेपमानां कृताञ्जलिम्।
verses.trans: O Rama having heard Rambha trembling in fear and standing with folded palms, Indra spoke:

verses.dev: माभैषी रम्भे भद्रं ते कुरुष्व मम शासनम्कोकिलो हृदयग्राही माधवे रुचिरद्रुमे।अहं कन्दर्पसहित स्स्थास्यामि तव पार्श्वत:
verses.trans: 'O Rambha have no fear. Feel safe. Carry out my order. When the trees look so beautiful in spring I shall in captivating the form of a cuckoo, stay by your side in the company of Kamadeva (Cupid).

words.m: in the company of Kandarpa, Cupid

verses.dev: माभैषी रम्भे भद्रं ते कुरुष्व मम शासनम्कोकिलो हृदयग्राही माधवे रुचिरद्रुमे।अहं कन्दर्पसहित स्स्थास्यामि तव पार्श्वत:
verses.trans: 'O Rambha have no fear. Feel safe. Carry out my order. When the trees look so beautiful in spring I shall in captivating the form of a cuckoo, stay by your side in the company of Kamadeva (Cupid).

words.m: in the company of Kandarpa, Cupid

verses.dev: त्वं हि रूपं बहुगुणं कृत्वा परमभास्वरम्।तमृषिं कौशिकं रम्भे भेदयस्व तपस्विनम्
verses.trans: O Rambha displaying many (romantic) gestures and assuming a fascinating form, distract the mighty ascetic, Kausika (Viswamitra)'.

verses.dev: सा श्रुत्वा वचनं तस्य कृत्वा रूपमनुत्तमम्।लोभयामास ललिता विश्वामित्रं शुचिस्मिता
verses.trans: At these words (of Indra), Rambha, assumed any form of excellent beauty and with a bright smile set out to allure Viswamitra.

verses.dev: कोकिलस्य च शुश्राव वल्गु व्याहरत: स्वनम्।सम्प्रहृष्टेन मनसा तत एनामुदैक्षत
verses.trans: Excited by the charming warble of the cuckoo, Viswamitra opened his eyes.

verses.dev: अथ तस्य च शब्देन गीतेनाप्रतिमेन च।दर्शनेन च रम्भाया मुनिस्सन्देहमागत:
verses.trans: On hearing the sound of the incomparable song of the cuckoo along with the sight of Rambha, a doubt arose in the mind of the ascetic.

verses.dev: सहस्राक्षस्य तत्कर्म विज्ञाय मुनिपुङ्गव:।रम्भां क्रोधसमाविष्ट श्शशाप कुशिकात्मज:
verses.trans: Son of Kushika, the eminent ascetic knew it was the work of Indra. Seized with anger, he cursed Rambha:

verses.dev: यन्मां लोभयसे रम्भे कामक्रोधजयैषिणम्।दशवर्षसहस्राणि शैली स्थास्यसि दुर्भगे
verses.trans: 'O luckless Rambha, since you have endeavoured to distract me from my desire to conquer wrath and passion, you shall turn into a rock and remain so for ten thousand years'.

words.m: with a desire to conquer wrath and passion

verses.dev: ब्राह्मण स्सुमहातेजा स्तपोबलसमन्वित:।उद्धरिष्यति रम्भे त्वां मत्क्रोधकलुषीकृताम्
verses.trans: 'O Rambha an effulgent brahmin, endowed with the power of asceticism, will liberate you from this state into which you have fallen on account of my anger'.

words.m: endowed with the power of asceticism
fouled on account of my anger

verses.dev: एवमुक्त्वा महातेजा विश्वामित्रो महामुनि:।अशक्नुवन् धारयितुं क्रोधं सन्तापमागत:
verses.trans: The brilliant Viswamitra, the great ascetic, now experienced remorse for his inability to contain anger.

verses.dev: तस्य शापेन महता रम्भा शैली तदाऽभवत्।वचश्शृत्वा च कन्दर्पो महर्षेस्स च निर्गत:
verses.trans: This mighty curse, of the great saint transformed Rambha into a rock and sent Kamadeva and Indra to their heels.

verses.dev: कोपेन स महातेजास्तपोऽपहरणे कृते।इन्द्रियैरजितै राम न लेभे शान्तिमात्मन:
verses.trans: "O Rama highly powerful Viswamitra was deprived of his ascetic merit due to his anger. He had no peace of mind for his inability to control his senses.

words.m: in deprivation of his ascetic merit

verses.dev: बभूवास्य मनश्चिन्ता तपोऽपहरणे कृते ।नैव क्रोधं गमिष्यामि न च वक्ष्ये कथञ्चन
verses.trans: Having been deprived of his ascetic merit, he resolved not to lose his temper nor ever breathe a word".

words.m: having been deprived of his ascetic merit

verses.dev: अथवा नोच्छवसिष्यामि संवत्सरशतान्यपि।अहं विशोषयिष्यामि ह्यात्मानं विजितेन्द्रिय:
verses.trans: "Further, I shall not even inhale for hundreds of years. I shall conquer my senses and dry up this body.

verses.dev: तावद्यावद्धि मे प्राप्तं ब्राह्मण्यं तपसाऽऽर्जितम्।अनुच्छवसन्नभुञ्जान स्तिष्ठेयं शाश्वतीस्समा:न हि मे तप्यमानस्य क्षयं यास्यन्ति मूर्तय:।
verses.trans: Until brahminhood is obtained by my austerities, I shall stay without breathing or eating for innumerable years. While performing penance, my limbs shall not undergo deterioration".

verses.dev: एवं वर्षसहस्रस्य दीक्षां स मुनिपुङ्गव:चकाराप्रतिमां लोके प्रतिज्ञां रघुनन्दन।
verses.trans: "O Descendant of Raghu in this way that eminent ascetic took an unprecedented vow to practise penance for a thousand years". इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये बालकाण्डे चतुष्षष्टितमस्सर्ग:।। Thus ends the sixtyfourth sarga of Balakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

words.m: religious practices for a thousand years
